summary:
As of February 2019, there is no evidence that major brain gangliosides are targeted in any FDA-approved or clinically advancing Alzheimer's disease therapies; instead, all prominent treatments focus exclusively on amyloid-beta clearance through monoclonal antibodies. Consequently, gangliosides remain outside the scope of currently developed therapeutic strategies.  

\boxed{B}

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
